statement.en: FATAL MUSCULOSKELETAL INJURY RISK DETERMINED USING ACCELEROMETER-BASED INERTIAL MEASUREMENT UNIT (IMU) SENSORS IN 28,481 THOROUGHBRED RACE STARTS
statement.summary: Objective: To determine whether screening of racing Thoroughbreds with accelerometer-based inertial measurement unit sensors (IMU) and a specifically trained algorithm identified most-at-risk horses for fatal musculoskeletal injury and whether age, gender, race distance, and track surface were associated with increased risk.

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: The axiom presents an empirical claim (predictive algorithm for fatal injury risk in racehorses) based on a large dataset (28,481 starts). However, the document is dated 2026-04-25 (future date relative to training cutoff) and marked as preprint. The methodology appears sound but requires peer validation, independent replication, and clinical implementation outcomes. The claim is not yet established as proven fact (TRUE) nor refuted (FALSE), and exists in provisional research state.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 加速度の乱れが死運命の確率を測る — 不可視の振動が運命を告げる
